title: Modular Arithmetic and Public-Key Reasoning · Goroutine Forensics
summary: An incident packet contains a plausible bounded modular arithmetic reasoning notebook with one primitive, parameter, nonce, context, key, parser, trust, timing, migration, or recovery defect; preserve the symptom and isolate first cause before repair. Planned artifact: Repair a changed failure in bounded modular arithmetic reasoning notebook and publish the reproduction, evidence, repair, and regression record. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal…
objectives: Compute and explain modular equivalence, reduction, addition, multiplication, exponentiation, and negative representatives.
Use Euclid and extended Euclid to determine coprimality and modular inverses and identify non-invertible cases.
Trace square-and-multiply cost and state why secret exponent code must use vetted constant-time implementations.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: Cryptography and Go Inspect the failure record · Debug Crypto Go Modular Congruence
steps.instruction:
debug case — A debug cryptographic engineering team handles case 1499 during a signed release review. Build deterministic Go evidence to normalize modular values and distinguish mathematical correctness from approved constant-time group execution; cap bytes, work, memory, time, attempts, concurrency, and retained secret material before use; then rotate the active key while old data remains readable and new writes cannot downgrade. Browser code uses pure state models or the narrow side-effect-free crypto allowlist with fixed fixtures. Production entropy and keys, secret custody, native timing and side channels, networks, TLS, HSM/KMS, certificate systems, FIPS or compliance state, load, faults, restore, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates. Competency probe: Compute and explain modular equivalence, reduction, addition, multiplication, exponentiation, and negative representatives.

Inspect the Crypto Go Modular Congruence trace for this debug case. Which record connects a precondition, observed state change, and boundary result?

Evidence target: Cryptography and Go Inspect the failure record · Debug Crypto Go Modular Congruence.
steps.why: The decision supplies observable evidence for compute and explain modular equivalence, reduction, addition, multiplication, exponentiation, and negative representatives. For Cryptography and Go Inspect the failure record · Debug Crypto Go Modular Congruence, keep Crypto Go Modular Congruence connected to executable Go behavior evidence.

steps.title: Cryptography and Go Implement the cause-level repair · Debug Crypto Go Toy Math Separation
steps.instruction:
Append a compile-ready pure-Go function headed "// Evidence: crypto-go-toy-math-separation-1itpgb" that uses case 9258 to normalize modular values and distinguish mathematical correctness from approved constant-time group execution. Return observable changed-case evidence. Browser code must not import cryptographic packages, generate or handle production keys, open sockets, contact TLS, HSM/KMS, certificate systems, compliance services, or external effects, read host state, execute host commands, or use credentials; verify those boundaries later with Go 1.26.5 compile, standard crypto APIs, authoritative vectors, fuzz, race, native side-channel measurement, interoperability, controlled network and key-custody systems, load, fault, recovery, compliance, and production gates.

Evidence target: Cryptography and Go Implement the cause-level repair · Debug Crypto Go Toy Math Separation.
steps.why: Building a runnable or mechanically reviewable artifact turns Crypto Go Toy Math Separation into observable performance evidence. For Cryptography and Go Implement the cause-level repair · Debug Crypto Go Toy Math Separation, keep Crypto Go Toy Math Separation connected to executable Go behavior evidence.

content.title: Evidence is more than syntax
content.text: The structural check is only one gate. The following changed-case decision and explanation must also hold.

hints: For Cryptography and Go Implement the cause-level repair · Debug Crypto Go Toy Math Separation, name the types, ownership, and stop path, Crypto Go Toy Math Separation precondition, or invariant that must hold.

At Cryptography and Go Implement the cause-level repair · Debug Crypto Go Toy Math Separation, trace this concrete input and predict values, errors, blocking, and output before reviewing it: A debug cryptographic engineering team handles case 7400 during a post-quantum compatibility canary. Build deterministic Go evidence to normalize modular values and distinguish mathematical correctness from approved constant-time group execution; reject toy, obsolete, unauthenticated, self-signed-trust, downgrade, and security-by-obscurity shortcuts; then remove one transfer gate and state exactly which production claim is no longer supported. Browser code uses pure state models or the narrow side-effect-free crypto allowlist with fixed fixtures. Production entropy and keys, secret custody, native timing and side channels, networks, TLS, HSM/KMS, certificate systems, FIPS or compliance state, load, faults, restore, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates. Competency probe: Keep small-number demonstrations outside production code and name the parameter, randomness, validation, and side-channel evidence they omit.

For Cryptography and Go Implement the cause-level repair · Debug Crypto Go Toy Math Separation, challenge the documented misconception with runtime, failure, race, or toolchain evidence: A toy RSA example is production-ready after increasing the integer values.
